metadata.notes: Values are selected from the Groomly prototype and T-023A audit. Near-neighbor prototype colors remain audit evidence; this JSON collapses them into conservative semantic tokens for later SwiftUI work. For T-023C, numeric px values from the mobile prototype should map 1:1 to SwiftUI pt; do not apply a second scale conversion.

colors.notes: Color values are extracted from Groomly.html or Groomly/Groomly.dc.html. Semantic token names are inferred from repeated usage and the T-023A audit.

appBackground.note: Use for the primary in-app background, not the outer device-frame backdrop.

surface.source: Repeated card, input, and button surfaces

surfaceRaised.source: Raised cards use white fill plus shadow
surfaceRaised.note: Elevation comes from the shadow token rather than a different fill.

border.source: Repeated input, outline button, and selected-card borders

borderSoft.source: Repeated softer card and icon-button borders

textSecondary.source: Body support copy, subtitles, and inactive tabs

textTertiary.source: Metadata, placeholders, disabled text, and inactive timeline labels

customerPrimary.source: Customer primary actions, progress, selected states, and active tabs

groomerAccent.source: Groomer mode accent, badges, and coral controls

success.source: Completed timeline dots and accepted/confirmed states

spacing.notes: Values are extracted from repeated gap, margin, and padding declarations. Scale names are inferred for SwiftUI token use. In T-023C, map these mobile prototype px values 1:1 to SwiftUI pt.

xs.source: Observed as small icon/text gap and minor padding

sm.source: Frequent gaps, label spacing, and compact vertical offsets

md.source: Frequent gaps and compact section spacing

lg.source: Frequent padding, field insets, and standard control spacing

xl.source: Screen/card spacing and major vertical offsets

screenHorizontal.source: Most authenticated screens use 20px horizontal padding

screenHorizontalLarge.source: Auth and role screens use 24px horizontal padding

radius.notes: Values are extracted from repeated border-radius declarations. In T-023C, map numeric px values 1:1 to SwiftUI pt. Treat chip=999 as a Capsule shape and circular=50% as a Circle shape rather than hard-coding those raw CSS values.

chip.swiftUIDirection: Use Capsule() or an equivalent capsule shape instead of hard-coding 999.

circular.source: Avatar, icon badge, and circular button radius
circular.swiftUIDirection: Use Circle() or an equivalent circular clip shape instead of translating 50% to a numeric radius.

shadow.notes: Shadow CSS values are extracted from repeated prototype declarations. SwiftUI mapping should preserve softness and low opacity rather than copy CSS syntax directly into views.

smallCard.source: Smaller pet and compact card shadow

typography.notes: Font sizes and weights are extracted from the prototype. SwiftUI semantic-style guidance is inferred from T-023A and the product design-system rule to prefer native SF/Dynamic Type. In T-023C, map numeric prototype px sizes 1:1 to SwiftUI pt only where fixed token sizes are explicitly introduced.

fontFamily.swiftUIDirection: Prefer native SF semantic text styles; use Inter only as web prototype evidence.

largeTitle.swiftUIDirection: Major screen titles; map conservatively to prominent SwiftUI title styling with bold weight.

title.swiftUIDirection: In-flow screen titles and wizard step titles.

headline.swiftUIDirection: Section headings and important card titles.
headline.source: Repeated section headings and card titles

body.swiftUIDirection: Primary and supporting body copy; use regular or medium weight based on hierarchy.
body.source: Frequent supporting copy and form text

caption.swiftUIDirection: Metadata and compact support text; use semibold only for chips, labels, or status text.
caption.source: Metadata, chip, label, and status text sizes
